label: Postman
description: Arcade.dev tools for interacting with Postman

tools.name: CreateCollectionFolder
tools.description: Add a folder to a collection, optionally nested inside an existing folder.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid to add the folder to.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the new folder.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the folder. Leave empty to omit.

parameters.name: parent_folder_id
parameters.description: Id of a folder to nest this folder inside. Leave empty to add at the top level.

output.description: The newly created folder.

description.value: Contains all authentication-related API requests including login, logout, and token refresh.

tools.name: CreateCollectionRequest
tools.description: Add a request to a collection, optionally inside a folder.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid to add the request to.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the new request.

parameters.name: http_method
parameters.description: HTTP method for the request.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Request URL.

parameters.name: folder_id
parameters.description: Id of a folder to place the request in. Leave empty to add at the top level.

output.description: The newly created request.

tools.name: CreateMock
tools.description: Create a mock server from a collection so a client can call its simulated endpoints.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of the collection the mock server simulates. Prefer the uid: it avoids a resolving lookup (useful in batches) and reliably addresses a teammate-shared collection, whose bare id can be rejected.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the mock server. Leave empty to let Postman name it.

parameters.name: environment_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of an environment to bind to the mock. Prefer the uid for a teammate-shared environment, whose bare id can be rejected. Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Workspace to create the mock in. Leave empty to use your personal (default) workspace; passing it avoids a default-workspace lookup, useful when creating many in a batch.

parameters.name: private
parameters.description: Create the mock as private (true), so only requests carrying a valid Postman API key reach it, or public (false), reachable by anyone with the URL. Defaults to true; a public mock exposes the collection's saved example responses to anyone with the URL.

output.description: The newly created mock server and its public URL.

tools.name: CreateMonitor
tools.description: Create a monitor that runs a collection on a schedule to watch an API's health.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of the collection the monitor runs. Prefer the uid: it avoids a resolving lookup (useful in batches) and reliably addresses a teammate-shared collection, whose bare id can be rejected.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the monitor.

parameters.name: cron
parameters.description: Cron expression for the run schedule (standard 5-field cron). Defaults to '0 0 * * *' (daily at midnight).

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone name for the schedule (e.g. America/New_York). Defaults to Etc/UTC.

parameters.name: environment_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of an environment to run the monitor against. Prefer the uid for a teammate-shared environment, whose bare id can be rejected. Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Workspace to create the monitor in. Leave empty to use your personal (default) workspace; passing it avoids a default-workspace lookup, useful when creating many in a batch.

output.description: The newly created monitor and its schedule.

tools.name: DeleteCollection
tools.description: Permanently delete a collection. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid to delete.

output.description: Confirmation that the collection was deleted.

tools.name: DeleteCollectionItem
tools.description:
Delete a folder or request from a collection. This cannot be undone.

Deleting a folder also removes the requests it contains.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid containing the item.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: Id of the folder or request to delete.

parameters.name: item_type
parameters.description: Whether the item is a folder or a request.

output.description: Confirmation that the folder or request was deleted.

tools.name: DeleteEnvironment
tools.description: Permanently delete an environment. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: environment_id
parameters.description: The environment id or uid to delete.

output.description: Confirmation that the environment was deleted.

tools.name: DeleteMock
tools.description: Permanently delete a mock server. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: mock_id
parameters.description: The mock server id to delete.

output.description: Confirmation that the mock server was deleted.

tools.name: DeleteMonitor
tools.description: Permanently delete a monitor. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: monitor_id
parameters.description: The monitor id or uid to delete.

output.description: Confirmation that the monitor was deleted.

tools.name: ForkCollection
tools.description: Fork a collection into a workspace as an independent, editable copy.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid to fork.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Label for the fork.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Workspace to create the fork in. Leave empty to use your personal (default) workspace; passing it avoids a default-workspace lookup, useful when creating many in a batch.

output.description: The forked copy of the collection.

tools.name: GetApi
tools.description: Inspect an API definition, including its name, summary, and attached schemas.

parameters.name: api_id
parameters.description: The API definition id.

output.description: The API definition with references to its schemas.

tools.name: GetApiSchema
tools.description: Read an API schema's files and their definition content.

parameters.name: api_id
parameters.description: The API definition id.

parameters.name: schema_id
parameters.description: The id of the schema to read.

output.description: The schema's files and their definition content.

tools.name: GetCollection
tools.description: Inspect a collection and return its variables and a flat tree of its folders and requests.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid. Prefer the uid for a collection shared by a teammate, whose bare id can be rejected.

output.description: The collection with its folders and requests.

tools.name: GetCollectionOpenapi
tools.description: Export a collection as an OpenAPI definition.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid to export.

output.description: The collection exported as an OpenAPI definition.

tools.name: GetEnvironment
tools.description: Inspect an environment and return its variables.

parameters.name: environment_id
parameters.description: The environment id or uid. Prefer the uid for an environment shared by a teammate, whose bare id can be rejected.

output.description: The environment and its variables.

tools.name: GetMock
tools.description: Inspect a mock server, including its public URL and the collection it is based on.

parameters.name: mock_id
parameters.description: The mock server id.

output.description: The mock server, its public URL, and its linked collection.

tools.name: GetMonitor
tools.description: Inspect a monitor, including its run schedule and most recent run result.

parameters.name: monitor_id
parameters.description: The monitor id or uid.

output.description: The monitor with its schedule and most recent run outcome.

tools.name: GetWorkspace
tools.description: Inspect a workspace and list the collections, environments, mocks, and monitors in it.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The unique workspace identifier.

output.description: The workspace and the resources it contains.

tools.name: ListApis
tools.description: List the API definitions in a workspace.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: The workspace whose API definitions to list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0. Paging to a deep offset re-scans the preceding results on this endpoint, so prefer a modest limit over paging far.

output.description: The API definitions in the workspace.

tools.name: ListCollections
tools.description:
List collections, optionally scoped to a workspace and filtered by name.

Without a workspace this returns the collections the API key can access: those you own or
have subscribed to. A collection another team member created in a shared workspace may not
appear here until it is subscribed to; use get_workspace to see everything a workspace holds.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Scope results to this workspace. Leave empty to span all accessible workspaces.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter to collections whose name contains this text. Leave empty for no filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: The collections matching the filters.

tools.name: ListEnvironments
tools.description: List environments, optionally scoped to a workspace and filtered by name.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Scope results to this workspace. Leave empty to span all accessible workspaces.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter to environments whose name contains this text. Leave empty for no filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: The environments matching the filters.

tools.name: ListMocks
tools.description: List mock servers, optionally scoped to a workspace and filtered by name.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Scope results to this workspace. Leave empty to span all accessible workspaces.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter to mock servers whose name contains this text. Leave empty for no filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: The mock servers matching the filters.

tools.name: ListMonitors
tools.description: List monitors, optionally scoped to a workspace.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Scope results to this workspace. Leave empty to span all accessible workspaces.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: The monitors matching the filters.

tools.name: ListWorkspaces
tools.description: List the workspaces the API key can access, optionally filtered by type.

parameters.name: workspace_type
parameters.description: Filter to one workspace type. Defaults to ALL (no filter).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: The workspaces accessible to the API key.

tools.name: RunMonitor
tools.description:
Trigger a monitor to run now and return its pass/fail results.

The run is synchronous: Postman holds the connection until the collection finishes. A run
that outlasts the tool's bounded wait returns ``timed_out=true`` while still executing
upstream; read the outcome from get_monitor's last-run fields rather than retrying.

parameters.name: monitor_id
parameters.description: The monitor id or uid to run.

output.description: The pass/fail outcome of the monitor run.

tools.name: SaveCollection
tools.description: Create a new collection, or update an existing one when collection_id is provided.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of the collection to update. Omit to create a new collection instead.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: When creating (no collection_id), the new collection's name. When updating, providing this renames the collection; omitting it leaves the name unchanged.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: When creating, sets the description. When updating, providing this changes it; omitting it leaves the description unchanged.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: When creating, the workspace to create the collection in; leave empty to use your personal (default) workspace (passing it avoids a default-workspace lookup, which matters when creating many in a batch). Ignored when updating an existing collection.

output.description: The created or updated collection.

description.value: A collection containing example API requests for testing the user authentication flow.

tools.name: SaveEnvironment
tools.description: Create a new environment, or update an existing one when environment_id is provided.

parameters.name: environment_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of the environment to update. Omit to create a new environment instead.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: When creating (no environment_id), the new environment's name. When updating, providing this renames it; omitting it leaves the name unchanged.

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: The environment's variables. When creating, seeds them (omit or pass an empty list for none). When updating, providing this replaces the full set; omitting it leaves the existing variables unchanged.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: When creating, the workspace to create the environment in; leave empty to use your personal (default) workspace (passing it avoids a default-workspace lookup, which matters when creating many in a batch). Ignored when updating an existing environment.

output.description: The created or updated environment.

tools.name: UpdateCollectionItem
tools.description: Rename a folder or request, or change a request's method or URL.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id or uid containing the item.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: Id of the folder or request to update.

parameters.name: item_type
parameters.description: Whether the item is a folder or a request.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: New name. Providing this renames the item; omitting it leaves the name unchanged.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: New description. Providing this changes it; omitting it leaves it unchanged.

parameters.name: http_method
parameters.description: New HTTP method (requests only). Omitting it leaves the method unchanged.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: New request URL (requests only). Omitting it leaves the URL unchanged.

output.description: The updated folder or request.

description.value: Fetches the profile details of a specific user by ID.

tools.name: UpdateEnvironmentVariables
tools.description: Set or remove individual environment variables without resending the whole variable set.

parameters.name: environment_id
parameters.description: Id or uid of the environment to edit.

parameters.name: set_variables
parameters.description: Variables to add or change; each one upserts by key, replacing that variable's value while leaving every other variable untouched. Omit for no additions or changes.

parameters.name: unset_keys
parameters.description: Keys of variables to remove. Keys that are not present are ignored. Omit to remove nothing.

output.description: The environment with its updated variables.

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description: Identify the Postman account the configured API key belongs to and its plan usage.

output.description: The Postman account the API key belongs to.

summary:
The Postman toolkit lets Arcade agents interact with the [Postman API](https://www.postman.com/) to manage collections, environments, mock servers, monitors, workspaces, and API definitions programmatically.

## Capabilities

- **Collection management** — create, inspect, export (OpenAPI), fork, save, and permanently delete collections; add, update, and delete folders and requests within a collection.
- **Environment management** — create, inspect, update individual variables, and delete environments.
- **Mock servers** — create mock servers backed by a collection, inspect them (including their public URL), and delete them.
- **Monitors** — create scheduled collection monitors, inspect run history and schedules, trigger on-demand runs with pass/fail results, and delete monitors.
- **Workspace and API discovery** — list and inspect workspaces, list collections/environments/mocks/monitors scoped to a workspace, list and inspect API definitions and their schemas.
- **Account introspection** — identify the authenticated Postman account and its plan usage.

## Secrets

`POSTMAN_API_KEY` — A Postman API key that authenticates every request made on behalf of a Postman account. To obtain one:
1. Log in to [Postman](https://www.postman.com/) and open **Settings → API keys** (direct link: [https://web.postman.co/settings/me/api-keys](https://web.postman.co/settings/me/api-keys)).
2. Click **Generate API Key**, give it a descriptive name, and copy the value immediately — it is shown only once.
3. The key inherits the permissions of the account that created it. For team workspaces, ensure the account has access to the workspaces and resources the toolkit needs to read or modify.
4. Postman's [API key documentation](https://learning.postman.com/docs/developer/postman-api/authentication/) describes key scopes, rotation, and revocation.

Store the key as an Arcade secret. See the [Arcade secrets guide](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) for configuration instructions, and manage your secrets at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
